title: Phase 12: Dispatcher — compose PathNetwork + EdgeDetector + BVPSmooth into unified IVP/BVP solver
description: Tier-3 composition layer. Stitches PathNetwork (pole-rich regions, IVP) + BVPSmooth (smooth regions, BVP) into one PadeTaylorRegionSolution covering an arbitrary 2D complex-plane domain. Inputs: a problem, a grid, and tunables. Outputs: per-grid-point (u, u', region_classification) where region is :ivp / :bvp / :uncovered. Architecture per docs/adr/0004-path-network-architecture.md (Tier-3 deferral) and docs/unified_path_network_spec.md §8. Edge-detector dependency: padetaylor-c2p. Newton-BVP dependency: BVP module shipped at commit cc7d8ca. Unblocks: figure_catalogue.md §6 Tier-3 figures (FW2011 Fig 4.1/4.5/4.6, FW2014 Fig 10/11/12/16/25, FW2015 Fig 7/8/9/11, RF2014 Fig 2/7/15).
notes: v1 SCOPE (committed 2026-05-13): 1D ordered-chain dispatcher composing IVPSegment <-> BVPSegment with junction derivative-match diagnostic per FW2011...md:192 (tol 1e-7). Public API: dispatch_solve(prob, f_bvp, df_du_bvp, segments; ...) -> DispatcherSolution. Each segment is IVPSegment(z_end) or BVPSegment(z_end, u_b, N). Callable sol(z) -> (u, u', region). Tests: DP.1.1 single-IVP passthrough, DP.1.2 single-BVP passthrough, DP.2.1 IVP->BVP junction derivative-match, DP.3.1 IVP->BVP->IVP three-segment (FW 2011 §4.4), DP.4.1 fail-fast guards, DP.5.1 mutation-proof. v2 DEFERRED: 2D lattice dispatcher with automatic edge detection (5-point Laplacian per FW2011...md:204-208, threshold 0.001), per-row 2D smooth-band filling, EdgeDetector module (padetaylor-c2p) consumed at that point. Asymptotic IC providers (InitialConditions module) deferred separately.
close_reason: Phase 12 v1 GREEN: Dispatcher.dispatch_solve composes IVPSegment + BVPSegment along 1D chain with junction derivative-match diagnostic per FW 2011 §4.4 + line 192.  329/329 tests GREEN; 3 mutations verified bite + restored.  v2 (2D lattice + edge detector) filed as padetaylor-k31.  See docs/worklog/007-phase-12-dispatcher.md.

title: Path-network module: 5-direction Stage 1 tree + Stage 2 fine grid per FW 2011 §3.1
description: Roll-our-own Julia module per FW2011...md:155-166 + RF2014 §3 explicit codification at RF2014...md:148-165. Stage 1: build coarse-grid path tree by 5-direction step probing (0°, ±22.5°, ±45° per FW2011, or ±15°, ±30° per RF2014 — reconcile in spec), pick min-|u| candidate per step, fixed h=0.5 with order=30 default. Random target ordering; restart from nearest visited node. Stage 2: single Padé-step extrapolation from each Stage-1 endpoint to fine-grid nodes. Composes with existing PadeStepper.pade_step_with_pade!. Open question: how to compose with BVP solver via edge-detector dispatch; see linked beads.
close_reason: PN.2.2 + PN.3.1 GREEN.  u(z=30) BF-256 rel-err = 2.13e-14 (beats FW 2011 published 8.34e-14).  Root cause: ADR-0004's canonical-Padé-per-node design was missed in Phase-10 commit 0ada60f — visited nodes k>1 stored wedge-direction Padé instead of real-h canonical Padé.  Two-line fix + 4-fail mutation E + 4-fail mutation F.  See docs/worklog/008-pathnetwork-tuning.md.

title: BVP module: Chebyshev-Newton spectral collocation per FW 2011 §3.2 / FFW 2017
description: Roll-our-own ~150 LOC Julia BVP solver. Spec: Chebyshev extrema nodes t_j=cos(jπ/N), analytic D₂ matrix (Weideman-Reddy / Trefethen SMIM ch.6), Newton iter with analytic Jacobian (PI: ∂F/∂u = 12u, diagonal mod of D₂), barycentric eval to fine lattice (Berrut-Trefethen). Generic in T<:Number for Float64 + BigFloat + Complex{T}; Arb routes through BigFloat for linear solve (mirrors LinAlg.pade_svd). Workflow: (1) acquire ground-truth refs Weideman-Reddy 2000 / Trefethen SMIM / Berrut-Trefethen 2004 / DMSUITE source, (2) build external/probes/bvp-oracle/capture.m using DMSUITE primitives in Octave for golden-master output, (3) RED tests against oracle, (4) port, (5) mutation-prove. Cite FW2011...md:176-200 and FFW2017...md:245.
close_reason: Phase 11 GREEN: BVP.bvp_solve shipped at commit cc7d8ca with 7 testsets + 3 mutations RED-confirmed. Standalone BVP solver + barycentric callable; Tier-3 figures unblocked pending Dispatcher composition.

title: v2 P0 — FW 2011 Table 5.1 long-range integration via path-network or post-pole adaptive step
description:
The original Phase-6 v1 acceptance (DESIGN.md §4 Phase 6 ORIGINAL,
pre-pivot) targeted FW 2011 Table 5.1 reference values:

  - u(30)     = 1.095098255959744                  rel err target 5e-13
  - u(10^4)   = 21.02530339471055                  rel err target 5e-10
  - u(28.261) = 9.876953517025014e6                rel err target 2e-9

Two v1 subagent attempts (fixed h_max + vault; step_jorba_zou-
adaptive + vault) plateaued at ~0.45 rel error at z=30.  Root cause
in worklog 004: FW's published numbers were generated by the
5-direction path-network (FW 2011 §3.1 + §5.3 lines 352-368) which
detours through the complex plane to dodge poles.  Real-axis fixed-h
stepping accumulates ~2 orders of magnitude of error per pole-vault;
~12 vaults in [0, 30] exhausts the budget.

v2 deliverables (one or both):

(a) Implement the FW path-network: 5-direction sampling per
    FW 2011 §3.1, picking the direction minimising |u(z)| at the
    candidate point.  Path-tree data structure for branching paths.
    Roughly DESIGN.md §5 'Real-axis-network integration' work item.

(b) Implement an adaptive step-size strategy that handles post-
    pole approach: after vaulting a pole, take a sequence of
    smaller h that keep the rescaled c̃'s dynamic range under
    control, then return to h_max once clear of the pole's
    influence.  step_pade_root from Phase 4 already gives the
    forward-pole-distance estimate; combine with a 'cool-down'
    factor that shrinks h in proportion to recent pole proximity.

Either approach should be tested against the three FW Table 5.1
reference values + the BigFloat-256 1e-65 stretch goal.

Reference values pinned in:
  - test/_oracle_problems.jl (already populated for FW Table 5.1)
  - external/probes/problems-oracle/ (capture scripts + verifier)
close_reason: Subsumed by PN.2.2.  u(z=30) BF-256 rel-err = 2.13e-14 verified GREEN (beats FW 2011 published 8.34e-14).  Float64 z=28.261 (high on pole wall) rel-err = 2.5e-5 — limited by pole proximity, separate concern (single-step-from-closer-node pattern as in Phase-6 test 6.1.3 if needed).  Float64 z=10000 deferred: runtime ~130s for ~20000 path-network steps; capability proven at z=30; quantitative confirmation can land as an optional/manual test if needed.  See worklog 008.

title: Phase 6 — Problems API + Padé-vs-Taylor demonstration (REVISED scope; see worklog 004)
description:
Phase 6 of Stage 2.  Public API: PadeTaylorProblem, solve_pade,
PadeTaylorSolution + dense interpolation.  Acceptance pivoted from
the original FW 2011 Table 5.1 long-range integration (v1-unreachable;
two subagent attempts reached 0.45 rel error vs spec 5e-13) to a
compelling demonstration of Padé-Taylor's analytic-continuation
advantage over plain Taylor, verified against three-source consensus
(closed-form WeierstrassP ≡ NDSolve ≡ mpmath.odefun).

FULL DETAILED SPEC: docs/worklog/004-phase-6-pivot.md

Test plan:
- ONE Padé built at z=0, h_max=1.5, spanning the ℘ pole at z=1.
- sol(0.5), sol(0.95), sol(1.05), sol(1.4) match closed-form ℘ at
  progressively-loosened tolerances.
- Side-by-side comparison: plain taylor_eval(coefs, 1.05) diverges
  while sol(1.05) tracks ℘.
- BigFloat-256 version of the demo.
- Mutation-proof.

Pre-existing infrastructure for the next agent:
- src/PadeStepper.jl exports pade_step_with_pade!(state, f, order, h)
  which returns (state, P_u) — useful for solve_pade's per-segment
  Padé storage.
- external/probes/problems-oracle/ has capture.wl/.py + verify.jl;
  needs extension with the new test points (z = 0.5, 0.95, 1.05, 1.4).
- test/_oracle_problems.jl is auto-generated; refresh after extending.

Long-range FW Table 5.1 reproduction is a separate P0 v2 bead
(file at session close).

title: Ship classical-Padé (Toeplitz \) as F64 default in RobustPade
description:
Phase-15 algorithmic improvement: ship FW 2011's classical Padé conversion (Toeplitz linear solve via \, per FW2011_*.md:346-350) alongside the current GGT 2013 SVD path.  Probe results (worklog 020):

| target | method            | wall (s) | rel-err  | vs FW Table 5.1 |
|--------|-------------------|----------|----------|------------------|
| z=30   | SVD (current)     | 5.84     | 6.6e-12  | 87× worse       |
| z=30   | classical (FW)    | 0.01     | 1.54e-13 | 2× worse        |
| z=10⁴  | SVD (current)     | 17.76    | 6.05e-6  | 25,800× worse   |
| z=10⁴  | classical (FW)    | 3.45     | 6.15e-11 | 3.8× BETTER     |

Classical is both more accurate AND 5-580× faster at F64 — GGT 2013 SVD does extra work (bidiagonalization, iterative diagonalization, tolerance-based degree-reduction) that's wasted on well-conditioned ℘-trajectory blocks.

Acceptance:
  (a) Add classical_pade_diagonal(c, m) to src/RobustPade.jl per FW2011_*.md:346-350.
  (b) Add a method-dispatch / kwarg on robust_pade so callers can choose :classical or :svd.  Default: :classical for T <: Union{Float32,Float64,Complex{Float32},Complex{Float64}} with :svd fallback on singular Toeplitz (per FW2011_*.md:346 'remove a row, min-norm solve'); :svd for BigFloat / Arb / generic AbstractFloat (GGT's robustness still load-bearing in arb-prec).
  (c) Mutation-prove the fallback path (force-singular Toeplitz to confirm SVD kicks in).
  (d) Tighten existing test acceptance to reflect the new accuracy: PN.2.2 z=30 F64 from 1e-9 → ~1e-12; PN.2.3 z=10⁴ F64 from 5e-5 → ~1e-10.
  (e) Worklog + ADR-0005 documenting the dispatch table + when each method is right.
  (f) Supersedes padetaylor-u7o (now concrete fix instead of open question).

References: docs/worklog/020 (TBD), references/markdown/FW2011_*.md:346-350, GGT 2013 §2.
close_reason: Closed

title: Phase 12 v2: 2D lattice dispatcher with automatic edge detection
description: Follow-up to Phase 12 v1 (1D-chain dispatcher) shipped at TBD-commit. v2 extends dispatch_solve to a 2D complex-plane lattice: (a) run PathNetwork over the full grid; (b) 5-point Laplacian classifier per FW2011...md:204-208 with threshold 0.001 to partition lattice cells into pole-field (IVP-stable) vs smooth (BVP-required); (c) per-row 1D BVP solves on contiguous smooth runs, with Dirichlet BCs from adjacent IVP cells (FW2011...md:190 'one BVP per grid line'); (d) stitched dense output with per-cell region tag. Acceptance: reproduce FW 2011 Fig 4.1 (near-tritronquée with smooth imaginary-axis band) per docs/figure_catalogue.md §1, target u(0), u'(0), u(20i), u'(20i) to <=1e-10 abs; pole locations agree with FW Table 5.1. Composes EdgeDetector (padetaylor-c2p) once that bead lands.
close_reason: Phase 12 v2 composition machinery GREEN. lattice_dispatch_solve composes path-network + EdgeDetector partition + per-row BVP fill per FW2011...md:190 horizontal-row pattern. 76 assertions (4 testsets), mutation-proven (Mutations E + F). Tested against cosh closed form to <=1e-10. FW Fig 4.1 quantitative pin (u(20i) <=1e-10) split into follow-up padetaylor-0c3 — Fig 4.1 uses a different compositional pattern (vertical BVP + two outward pole fields), not the line-190 horizontal-row algorithm shipped here. See worklog 013.

title: PathNetwork PN.2.2 + PN.3.1: FW Table 5.1 quantitative accuracy + :steepest_descent ≡ :min_u
description: Follow-up tuning for PathNetwork (Phase 10) shipped at commit 0ada60f. PN.2.2 deferred per ADR-0004's v1 split: at present the path-network walks correctly to z=1.4 past the lattice pole, but the final boundary-of-disc evaluation accumulates ~25% rel-err because the walk takes a wide off-axis detour and the last visited node's canonical Padé extrapolates poorly back to the on-axis target. Tuning levers to investigate: (a) h smaller (0.3 instead of 0.5) for more sample density per pole-radius; (b) order higher (40 instead of 30) for BF-256 the (15,15) approx-error floor lifts; (c) per-step direction-specific Padé instead of canonical-direction storage; (d) Stage-2 evaluation from multiple visited nodes weighted by distance; (e) the :steepest_descent variant per FW 2011 §5.4.1 line 362-368. PN.3.1 (:steepest_descent ≡ :min_u to ≤1e-10 on PN.1.1's tree) is a small test once :min_u tuning is complete. Acceptance: u(z=30) matches u_at_30_FW_ref to ≤1e-13 (BF-256) per docs/figure_catalogue.md §1 row FW2011 Fig 5.1; ≤1e-9 Float64.
close_reason: PN.2.2 + PN.3.1 GREEN.  u(z=30) BF-256 rel-err = 2.13e-14 (beats FW 2011 published 8.34e-14).  Root cause: ADR-0004's canonical-Padé-per-node design was missed in Phase-10 commit 0ada60f — visited nodes k>1 stored wedge-direction Padé instead of real-h canonical Padé.  Two-line fix + 4-fail mutation E + 4-fail mutation F.  See docs/worklog/008-pathnetwork-tuning.md.

title: Edge detector: 5-point Laplacian classifier per FW 2011 §3.2.2
description: Roll-our-own ~30 LOC module per FW2011...md:204-208. 5-point finite-difference stencil [1,1,-4,1,1] for ∇²u on equispaced complex lattice; classify lattice point as pole-field if |∇²u|/h² > 0.001 threshold (FW's empirical level). Output: bitmap on the lattice, fed to the IVP/BVP dispatcher. Note FW2011...md:401: in published runs they classified manually after inspection rather than fully automating; v1 may follow suit and offer the detector as a diagnostic tool first.
close_reason: EdgeDetector v1 shipped (60-LOC module, 743 assertions, 2 mutations). FW 2011 §3.2.2 eq. (3.3) verbatim. Threshold on log10|Δu| per FW Fig 3.3 (not bare |Δu|; corrected from bead description). See worklog 011.

title: FFW 2017 Fig 5: PIII tronquée + condition-number heatmap
description: FFW 2017 Fig 5 (md:240-265): PIII (α,β,γ,δ)=(1,-1/20,0,-1), two boundary ICs at z=30 e^{±(9π/4-π/12)i} (md:240-244). Pole-free sector -3π/2 < Im ζ < 9π/2 with 5 strips. Two panels: (i) ζ-plane |w(ζ)| modulus showing tronquée structure; (ii) condition-number heatmap log10(κ) where κ_r = 27/16 |z|^{4/3} (md:262), reaching ~157 at z=30 (md:264). Uses IVPBVPHybrid driver from A6 (commit c41eaa2). Push n_terms ≥ 15 in asymptotic series to escape FFW md:226 baseline. Acceptance: per-strip errors 4e-6 / 3e-7 / 2e-8 / 3e-9 / 4e-8 within 1 order; condition number κ_r ≈ 157 at boundary. Worklog 040. Step B4.

Two-panel render: Panel A |w(ζ)| over principal-sheet sub-strip via the IVP+BVP hybrid driver (A6); Panel B closed-form κ_r heatmap over full FFW sector. Tests 2197 → 2220 GREEN (+23). All mutations M1/M3/M4 bite (M2 v1 no-op). v1 corner: FFW md:240 per-strip 1e-7 - 1e-8 errors NOT reached (achieved ~1e-2, 4 orders looser); follow-up beads padetaylor-ykg (a_3+ coefficients) + padetaylor-5k9 (sheet-aware API) opened for the v2 lift. Commit f05f13e.

title: BVP.jl supports u' in the RHS for P̃_III hybrid driver
description: Existing bvp_solve(f, ∂f_∂u, ...) assumes f(z, u). P̃_III has f(z, w, w') = (w')²/w + (1/4)(αw² + γw³ + βe^ζ + δe^{2ζ}/w). For the hybrid driver to work on P̃_III (FFW md:43, md:245), bvp_solve was extended minimally and additively to accept a 3-arg f(z, u, u') + 3-arg ∂f_∂u + ∂f_∂up. Implemented via dispatch on number of methods. Documented as part of padetaylor-0co; this bead is the standing artefact.

title: IVP+BVP hybrid driver for pole-free sectors (FFW 2017 §3)
description: FFW 2017 md:203-245 algorithm pairing Padé IVP (PFS) with Chebyshev spectral BVP on pole-free sectors. New src/IVPBVPHybrid.jl module: (1) run PFS from two asymptotic boundary points on the pole-free sector's bounding rays, (2) harvest u, u' values on both curved boundaries, (3) call bvp_solve with those as BCs, (4) glue solutions. Asymptotic-series helper for u ~ z^{1/3}·[1 + Σ a_n z^{-2n/3}] (md:230) as IC source. Needed for FFW Fig 5 (B4). ADR-0014. Worklog 039. Step A6 of 11-step plan.
close_reason: Shipped IVP+BVP hybrid driver per FFW 2017 §3 (new module src/IVPBVPHybrid.jl). Closed-form a_1 = -β/3, a_2 = ((4/9) + δ a_1²)/(2δ). Additive BVP 3-arg-RHS overload (artefact bead padetaylor-i76). Tests 2131 → 2197 GREEN (+66). M1-M4 mutations all bite. Commit c41eaa2.

title: lattice_dispatch_solve throws on plain-IVP-corrupted BCs in smooth sectors
description:
SYMPTOM
lattice_dispatch_solve on PI tritronquée at N=121 over [-20,20]² (h_grid=0.333, h_path=0.5, N_bvp=20) throws on the first non-convergent per-row BVP: Newton final ‖Δu‖_∞=0.4458 vs tol=1.8e-12, ‖R‖_∞=169.5 after 10 iterations.  Reproducible via /tmp/smoke_lattice_dispatch.jl from the padetaylor-f8l smoke session.

ROOT CAUSE
Step 1 of lattice_dispatch_solve uses plain path_network_solve over the full grid (no edge gating).  In the pole-free angular sectors (4 of 5 for the tritronquée per FW2011...md:52-54), the plain IVP is walk-tree-seam-corrupted (md:401: 'smooth regions are unstable regions for any IVP solver').  Step 3 then BVP's per-row smooth runs using those corrupted IVP cells as endpoint BCs.  Between two corrupted endpoints across a 30+ unit smooth span there is no self-consistent BVP solution; Newton diverges; lattice_dispatch_solve throws fail-loud (BVP.jl:305).

The throw IS Rule-1-correct fail-loud; the bug is that the lattice composition uses a known-corrupted source as BCs.  Same recipe works fine for FW Fig 4.1 (figures/fw2011_fig_4_1.jl) because there the BVP-spine + IVP-runouts geometry gives clean BCs — the spine is BVP'd, not IVP'd.

PROPOSED FIX
(a) Use edge_gated_pole_field_solve as the IVP source instead of plain path_network_solve.  The gated IVP gives clean values at the wedge boundary cells, which are the only ones used as BVP BCs (since the BVP runs over smooth segments flanked by mask=true).  Mask=true cells are inside the edge-gated field by construction.
(b) Fail-soft on BVP non-convergence: tag the run as :bvp_fail in region_tag, leave the cells at their input-IVP value, continue.  The caller can then choose: throw on any :bvp_fail, ignore, or retry with higher N_bvp.  Add a strict::Bool kwarg defaulting to true (current behavior) for opt-in.

AFFECTED
src/LatticeDispatcher.jl (the path_network_solve call at line ~204; the bvp_solve call at line ~256; the region_tag enum needs :bvp_fail).  Existing tests in test/lattice_dispatcher_test.jl (FF.2.* style) must stay GREEN.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- lattice_dispatch_solve on PI tritronquée at N=121/[-20,20]² returns a LatticeSolution (no throw) under default strict=false-equivalent behavior.
- region_tag distinguishes :bvp (converged), :bvp_fail (non-convergent, left at IVP), :ivp_only (smooth, no BVP attempted).
- New test: smoke-style regression on the tritronquée reproducer.
- Existing fw_fig_41 + lattice_dispatcher tests stay GREEN, mutation-proven.

Refs: docs/worklog/032-edge-detector-h-scaling.md (the smoke session that surfaced this); src/LatticeDispatcher.jl:204+256; src/BVP.jl:305; references/markdown/FW2011_painleve_methodology_JCP230/FW2011_painleve_methodology_JCP230.md:401.

title: EdgeDetector level threshold is grid-fixed; must scale as level₀ + 2·log₁₀(h/h₀)
description:
SYMPTOM
edge_gated_pole_field_solve region-growing stalls at any lattice spacing finer than ~0.25 — the field never grows past the seed disk. Empirically (PI tritronquée, [-20,20]², grow_rings tuned to a ~1-unit frontier):
  h=0.25  (N=161): level=0.001 -> 24 passes, ~18% field  OK
  h=0.125 (N=321): level=0.001 -> STALL (2 passes, seed only); level≈-0.6 -> 21 passes ~18% OK
  h=0.0625(N=641): level=0.001 -> STALL; level=-0.5 -> STALL; level=-1.0 -> 20 passes ~18% OK
The working levels match level = 0.001 + 2·log10(h/0.25) almost exactly (h=0.125 -> -0.60; h=0.0625 -> -1.20).

ROOT CAUSE
EdgeDetector.pole_field_mask thresholds log10|Δu| > level with a fixed default level=0.001; EdgeGatedSolve.edge_gated_pole_field_solve and LatticeDispatcher.lattice_dispatch_solve pass it through as edge_level. But Δu = (5-point stencil · u)/h² of a holomorphic (∴ harmonic) function is O(h²) away from poles — and crucially the pole-*annulus* residual that the flood-fill relies on for cell-to-cell connectivity ALSO scales ~h² (only cells essentially on a pole, d~h, stay ~1/h⁴). So the smooth/annulus floor of log10|Δu| scales as 2·log10(h) + const. A fixed level therefore over-classifies as smooth on fine grids: below spacing 0.25 the connecting annulus drops under 10^level, the flood-fill cannot propagate between adjacent poles, and region growing stalls at the seed.

FW 2011 used a single fixed grid resolution, so a constant 0.001 was correct for them (references/markdown/FW2011_painleve_methodology_JCP230/FW2011_painleve_methodology_JCP230.md:202-208 — '...proportionality constant in the O(h²)...'). The bug is in OUR generalization: we exposed h as a free kwarg but kept level constant. src/EdgeDetector.jl's docstring half-acknowledges this ('calibrated for fine grids', 'Grid resolution matters') but punts it to the caller instead of fixing the scaling — a CLAUDE.md Rule 2 'all bugs are deep' item, not a 'pass edge_level manually' band-aid.

PROPOSED FIX
Make level h-aware: pole_field_mask's default level becomes LEVEL0 + 2·log10(h/H0) with the empirically-calibrated point (H0,LEVEL0)=(0.25,0.001). Keep an explicit level kwarg as an override (e.g. level=:auto sentinel vs a passed Real). Thread the same through edge_gated_pole_field_solve's edge_level and lattice_dispatch_solve. Ground-truth check: confirm FW's own Fig 3.3 grid spacing if recoverable; otherwise our (0.25,0.001) calibration stands and should be documented as such.

AFFECTED
src/EdgeDetector.jl (pole_field_mask), src/EdgeGatedSolve.jl (edge_level passthrough), src/LatticeDispatcher.jl (edge_level), figure scripts hardcoding grids. BLOCKS: the high-resolution headline render (examples/tritronquee_3d.jl at N=641 for a 4x-resolution README hero image) — deferred until this is fixed. The README currently keeps the working N=161 image.
design: Δu smooth-region floor ~ h²; pole-annulus (connectivity-bearing) residual also ~ h²; only d~h cells stay ~1/h⁴. ∴ log10|Δu| floor ∝ 2·log10(h). Fix: level(h) = LEVEL0 + 2·log10(h/H0), calibrated (H0,LEVEL0)=(0.25,0.001) — confirmed by the 3-point sweep above.
acceptance_criteria: edge_gated_pole_field_solve grows correctly (>5 passes, field reaches the expected ~18% tritronquée wedge) at h ∈ {0.25, 0.125, 0.0625} with the DEFAULT level (no manual edge_level); existing EdgeDetector/EdgeGatedSolve/LatticeDispatcher tests stay GREEN, mutation-proven; FW figures 3.3 / 4.1 / 4.7 still reproduce; examples/tritronquee_3d.jl re-renders cleanly at N=641.
close_reason: h-aware auto-scaled level shipped (HEAD 17c284c). 1652/1652 GREEN. N=641 hero render finished cleanly: 92 passes, 75,646-cell field (18.41%), wall 79 min. ADR-0009, worklog 032.

title: Edge-gated pole-field solve — confine IVP path-network to pole fields (FW md:401)
description: FW 2011 md:401: 'smooth regions are unstable regions for any IVP solver ... force the path selection algorithm to complete pole fields before stepping into smooth regions ... with the pole field edge detection procedure (3.2.2) this process can readily be fully automated.' Confirmed empirically: ungated path_network_solve over large regions corrupts the PI tritronquee (spurious poles leak into the 4 pole-free sectors); restricting targets to pole-field cells gives a perfectly sector-confined result. Build edge_gated_pole_field_solve: region-growing solve that starts from a seed near the IC and iteratively expands the targeted cell set ONLY into cells the EdgeDetector (FW 3.2.2) classifies as pole-field, re-running path_network_solve each ring. The IVP never targets deep-smooth cells, so the visited Pade store stays faithful and extract_poles gives a clean pole field. Composes PathNetwork + EdgeDetector. Needed for FW Fig 4.7 panel (f) tritronquee (bead padetaylor-7na).
design: New module src/EdgeGatedSolve.jl. edge_gated_pole_field_solve(prob, xs, ys; h, order, edge_level, seed_radius, grow_rings, max_iter) -> EdgeGatedSolution{T} carrying the field BitMatrix + the confined PathNetworkSolution (extract_poles(sol.pn_solution) for the scatter). Region-growing: dilate field by grow_rings, solve, pole_field_mask, accept newly-classified pole-field cells, repeat to fixpoint. BVP-fill of BOUNDED smooth bands stays with lattice_dispatch_solve; the tritronquee's smooth sectors are UNBOUNDED (correctly left empty for a pole-location plot).

title: PoleField: extract pole locations from a solved PathNetwork
description: Capability gap flagged in HANDOFF.md: FW 2011 Fig 4.7/4.8 (pole-location plots) need a helper that extracts pole locations from a PathNetworkSolution. Each visited node stores a local Padé approximant P(t)=N(t)/D(t) in the rescaled variable t=(z-z_v)/h; its poles in the z-plane are z_v + h·t_root for roots t_root of D(t). The helper takes the union over all visited nodes, filters spurious/far roots, and deduplicates. Ground truth oracle: the equianharmonic Weierstrass-℘ test problem (u''=6u², FW §5.1.1) whose poles sit on the analytic rhombic lattice z(m,n)=1+2ω(m+n/2)+i·ω√3·n — extracted poles must match this lattice to tolerance. Unblocks the Fig 4.7/4.8 figure reproduction.
design: New module src/PoleField.jl. extract_poles(sol::PathNetworkSolution; radius_t, dedup_atol) -> Vector{Complex{T}}. Uses Polynomials.roots on the denominator b of each visited_pade entry (validated for Complex{Float64}). robust_pade's GGT-2013 SVD rank detection already suppresses Froissart doublets. Radius/dedup defaults pinned empirically against the ℘ lattice per worklog-7 lesson.
acceptance_criteria: extract_poles on a ℘-problem path_network_solve recovers the known rhombic-lattice poles within the covered region to <=1e-6 abs; no spurious poles; mutation-proven (perturb root mapping -> RED).
notes: Shipped src/PoleField.jl + test/polefield_test.jl (22 assertions, mutation-proven M1/M2/M3). extract_poles maps each visited node's Padé denominator roots to the z-plane (z=z_v+h*t*), filters by radius_t/min_residue/cross-node-support, clusters greedily in increasing |t*|. Verified vs equianharmonic ℘ lattice: in-region poles to ~1e-8 (figure-catalogue Float64 spec is 1e-6). Worklog 027. Suite 1487->1509 GREEN. Two findings: double poles split under finite-order Padé (handled by cluster_atol); genuine out-of-region poles are honestly reported but under-resolved (caller clips to display window, as FW does).

title: PathNetwork: add enforce_real_axis_symmetry kwarg
description: path_network_solve's shuffle(rng, targets) breaks y-symmetry of the visited tree even when the ODE has real coefficients and ICs are real. For such cases the mathematically correct behaviour is u(z̄) = ū(z), but Stage-2 nearest-visited lookup landing on non-conjugate nodes cascades 4-5 orders of magnitude into |u| at conjugate-pair grid cells (worklog 014, examples/tritronquee_3d.jl shows the fix in user space). Add an opt-in kwarg  that: (a) filters targets to upper-half + on-axis; (b) walks those only; (c) populates lower-half grid_u/grid_up via conj(grid_u_upper). Bit-exact y-symmetric; ~2× faster on full-plane fills. Inapplicable for non-real ICs / complex-coefficient ODEs — leave as opt-in.

title: Friction recorded — GenericLinearAlgebra pirates into LinearAlgebra.svd!
description: When using GenericLinearAlgebra: GenericLinearAlgebra in scope, LinearAlgebra.svd(A::Matrix{BigFloat}) actually works because GenericLinearAlgebra adds methods to LinearAlgebra.svd! directly (a form of type piracy that's intentional in their design). title: 2D elliptic Laplace BVP for analytic smooth-region fill
description:
CONTEXT
The Painlevé transcendents are complex-analytic in their pole-free sectors.  Any complex-analytic function u(z) over the (x,y) plane satisfies ∇²u = 0 (the real 2D Laplacian of its real and imaginary parts both vanish — Cauchy-Riemann).  So in a pole-free smooth region with Dirichlet BCs on its boundary, u(x,y) is the unique harmonic function with those BCs.  This is a classical 2D elliptic BVP, fundamentally cleaner than the per-row 1D BVP approach (sibling bead padetaylor-8dg).

This bead is the more principled alternative to padetaylor-8dg.  Both produce a filled smooth region from boundary data; the 2D version is globally consistent (the per-row version can have inter-row discontinuities at the wedge boundary), handles corner-shaped sector geometry naturally (the per-row version requires bilateral flanking), and reduces to a single sparse linear solve per smooth connected component.

PROPOSED API
New function (probably src/SmoothFill2D.jl):

  smooth_fill_2d(u_grid, mask, xs, ys; outer_bc) -> Matrix{Complex{T}}

  - u_grid :: Matrix{Complex{T}}  -- input grid; mask=true cells are taken as given (Dirichlet BC), mask=false cells overwritten
  - mask   :: BitMatrix           -- true = boundary/source (from edge-gated IVP), false = unknown
  - outer_bc :: (z) -> u OR :asymptotic_PI etc. -- BC at the grid edge cells that are mask=false

Algorithm:
  - For each connected component of mask=false cells:
    * Identify boundary cells (neighbours of mask=true cells AND grid-edge cells).
    * Assemble sparse 5-point Laplace operator on the interior cells.
    * RHS from Dirichlet BCs (mask=true neighbours from u_grid; grid-edge from outer_bc).
    * Solve once via sparse LU.  Fill the interior cells with the result.
  - Returns the full grid with all cells populated.

The same machinery generalises to any complex-analytic ODE solution.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- PI tritronquée at [-20,20]² rendered with smooth_fill_2d:
  * Continuous |u| values across the wedge boundary (no harsh -1 floor; no walk-tree seams).
  * Asymptotic recovered at grid edges with outer_bc=:asymptotic_PI.
  * Visually clean — Painlevé-2-style smooth gradient across the four pole-free sectors.
- New test/smooth_fill_2d_test.jl with mutation-proven assertions.
- Headline render examples/tritronquee_3d.jl uses smooth_fill_2d for the smooth sectors.

REJECTED ALTERNATIVES
- 'Per-row 1D BVP (padetaylor-8dg)' — simpler but inter-row inconsistent and can't handle non-bilateral runs.  Keep as a lower-cost fallback.
- 'Plain path_network_solve over the whole grid' — has walk-tree seams (demonstrated in /tmp/smoke_plain_full_solve.png from the padetaylor-f8l smoke session).
- 'Lattice dispatcher with corrected BCs' — its per-row geometry can't fill the half-bounded outer rim regardless of BC quality.

DEPENDENCIES
- Useful but not required: padetaylor-8dg's asymptotic factories (asymptotic_PI, asymptotic_PII, ...) — if shipped first, smooth_fill_2d can reuse them rather than reimplementing.
- Independent of: padetaylor-f8l (the EdgeDetector fix), edge_gated_pole_field_solve (this consumes its output, doesn't modify it).

NOTES
- Probably the right long-term answer for headline renders of any Painlevé transcendent.  PII / PIV / etc. just plug in their own asymptotic.
- Performance: a single sparse LU on the smooth-component cells.  At N=641 / [-20,20]² with ~80% smooth coverage that's ~330k unknowns — well within sparse-direct-solver capacity (LU on a 5-point stencil graph is essentially free).

Refs: padetaylor-8dg (1D per-row variant, the lighter alternative); padetaylor-f8l worklog 032 (the smoke session that surfaced the gap); src/EdgeGatedSolve.jl (the IVP source for the inner BCs); references/markdown/FW2011_painleve_methodology_JCP230/FW2011_painleve_methodology_JCP230.md:42-55 (PI asymptotic).

title: bvp_fill_with_asymptotic: 1D per-row BVP using algebraic asymptotic as outer BC
description:
CONTEXT
Most smooth-sector rows in a tritronquée-style headline render are NOT bilaterally flanked by IVP cells — they reach the grid edge on one (or both) sides with the smooth/pole-free sector wide open.  The existing per-row bvp_solve in lattice_dispatch_solve needs BCs at both ends and so leaves those cells either at their (corrupted) plain-IVP values or, post the sibling bug fix, tagged :ivp_only.

The Painlevé equations have known leading-term algebraic asymptotics in their pole-free sectors that act as natural outer BCs.  For Painlevé I, the Boutroux asymptotic is u(z) ~ -sqrt(-z/6) at large |z| in the pole-free regime (FW2011...md:42-55, used in figures/fw2011_fig_4_1.jl step (i) as the leading() function).  PII, PIV have their own (FW2014 etc.).

PROPOSED API
New function (probably src/AsymptoticFill.jl, or as a new method on bvp_solve):

  bvp_fill_with_asymptotic(prob, asymptotic, xs, ys; mask, inner_bcs, N_bvp=20) -> Matrix{Complex{T}}

  - asymptotic :: (z) -> u  -- the algebraic asymptotic for the equation (PI: z -> -sqrt(-z/6))
  - mask       :: BitMatrix -- true = pole-field (skip), false = smooth (fill)
  - inner_bcs  :: Matrix{Complex{T}} -- IVP values at mask=true cells (from edge_gated)

For each smooth row j:
  - Find contiguous false-runs in mask[:, j]
  - For each run [i_lo, i_hi]:
    * Outer BCs: asymptotic(z(i_lo, j)) or inner_bcs[i_lo-1, j] (whichever exists)
    * Inner BCs: similarly on the high end
    * bvp_solve over the run
  - Returns the filled u_grid (smooth cells now BVP-resolved)

For PII / PIV / other Painlevés the same shape generalises — just plug in the relevant asymptotic.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- PI tritronquée at [-20,20]² rendered with bvp_fill_with_asymptotic over the four pole-free sectors:
  * Values continuous across the wedge boundary (rel_err < 1e-4 vs edge-gated IVP at boundary cells).
  * Asymptotic recovered at grid edge (rel_err < 1e-6 vs asymptotic(z) at outer cells).
- New test/asymptotic_fill_test.jl with mutation-proven assertions.
- Demo figure: examples/tritronquee_3d.jl re-rendered with the smooth sectors BVP-filled (no -1 display floor, no walk-tree seams).

REJECTED ALTERNATIVES
- 'Use a coarser BVP grid' — doesn't help, the issue is missing one BC, not resolution.
- '2D elliptic BVP for the whole sector' — sibling bead, more principled but more work.  This bead is the lighter-weight variant.

NOTES
- This is purely additive (new function); no impact on existing IVP / lattice_dispatch / edge_gated APIs.
- Couples loosely with the asymptotic functions already in figures/fw2011_fig_4_1.jl — that script's leading(z) = -sqrt(-z/6) is the per-equation factory we'd lift into a public asymptotic_PI helper.

Refs: figures/fw2011_fig_4_1.jl (existing 1D asymptotic-BC example); references/markdown/FW2011_painleve_methodology_JCP230/FW2011_painleve_methodology_JCP230.md:42-55.

title: Closed-form named Painleve families: PII rational/Airy, PIV entire (exact oracles)
description: Follow-on to the named-transcendent constructors (bead for tritronquee/hastings_mcleod). The in-tree references give EXACT closed forms for several parametrized Painleve solution families (worklog 031 reconnaissance): PII rational solutions u_1=-1/z, u_2, u_3 (FW2014 md:114-127; u_3 has IC (0,0), u_1/u_2 have a pole at z=0 so need a non-zero IC point); PII Airy solutions for alpha=n+1/2 via an Airy phi (FW2014 md:130-167); PIV entire solutions u=-2z, u=-(2/3)z (RF2014 md:89-92). These differ in shape from the point-IC named transcendents: they are parametrized families (pii_rational(n), pii_airy(theta)) AND they come with EXACT closed-form oracles, which makes them ideal self-validating tests (solver output cross-checked against the exact formula). Deferred from v1 because the parametrized-family API + closed-form-oracle test pattern deserves its own ADR treatment. Out of scope: PIV Generalized Hermite/Okamoto rationals (formulas not in-tree, cite Clarkson 2006).
close_reason: Closed-form parametrised Painlevé families shipped. 7 new constructors (pii_rational n∈{1,2,3}, pii_airy n∈{0,1}, piv_entire {:minus_2z, :minus_two_thirds_z}). 1652 → 1708 GREEN (+56 CF.* assertions). ADR-0010 + worklog 033. New runtime dep: SpecialFunctions.  Deferred to follow-up: u_{5/2}, n≥4 Bäcklund rationals, PIV Hermite/Okamoto.

title: PainleveProblem :transformed forwarding methods
description: v1 of the PainleveProblem layer (ADR-0006, bead padetaylor-avl) ships forwarding methods (solve_pade, path_network_solve) that work transparently for :direct equations (PI/PII/PIV) but THROW for :transformed equations (PIII/PV/PVI). Reason: a ζ-frame PathNetworkSolution cannot be faithfully round-tripped — the per-node Padé store and step length h are intrinsically ζ-frame and have no z-frame image. This bead: implement :transformed forwarding that maps the input grid z→ζ and maps the point-value fields (grid_z/u/up, visited_z/u/up) back through pp.from_frame, documenting that visited_pade + visited_h stay ζ-frame. Ref worklog 026, src/Painleve.jl.
close_reason: Subsumed by ADR-0007 / bead padetaylor-p1b: PainleveSolution carries to_frame/from_frame and does z-frame point-value remapping for :transformed problems; the forwarding methods no longer throw.

title: path_network_solve ignores prob.order (API footgun)
description: path_network_solve(prob, grid; order=30, ...) carries its own order kwarg and never reads prob.order, even though PadeTaylorProblem has an order field. Passing order to the problem constructor silently has no effect on the path-network solve — caught while writing figures/fw2011_fig_5_2.jl (an (order,h) sweep that was accidentally all-order-30). Options: (a) default the kwarg to prob.order, (b) drop the kwarg and read prob.order, (c) document loudly. Check Dispatcher/LatticeDispatcher for the same pattern. Ref worklog 025.
close_reason: path_network_solve / dispatch_solve / lattice_dispatch_solve order kwargs now default to prob.order (resp. prob_ivp.order) instead of a hard-coded 30. Regression tests PN.7.1 / DP.1.3 / LD.2.2 added, all three mutation-proven. Suite 1432→1439 GREEN.

title: Quantitative pole-count pin for FW 2011 Fig 4.3/4.4
description: Add a test pinning the FW 2011 Fig 4.3/4.4 acceptance from docs/figure_catalogue.md: pole count over the [-10,10]^2 window for PI u(0)=0 u'(0)=1.8518 vs FW; pole positions ≤1e-6 abs; and the count for u'(0)=1.8519 differs from the 1.8518 case by exactly one front-row pole (the tronquée-transition signature, FW2011_*.md:233,251). Figure scripts figures/fw2011_fig_4_3.jl + fw2011_fig_4_4.jl already render the surfaces (worklog 024); this is the test-side companion. Could use EdgeDetector.pole_field_mask + connected-component counting.

title: F64 long-range accuracy: close the 600-25000× gap vs FW Padé
description: At Float64 z=30 we get rel-err 4.46e-11 vs FW's 7.62e-14 (600×); at z=10⁴ we get 6.05e-6 vs FW's 2.34e-10 (25,000×). BF-256 we BEAT FW (worklog 008), so algorithm is correct — gap is F64-specific roundoff. Worklog 019 enumerates attack vectors: (1) route F64 through GenericLinearAlgebra Jacobi SVD instead of LAPACK Demmel-Kahan; (2) Kahan-summed Horner in _evaluate_pade / _evaluate_pade_deriv; (3) compensated h^k accumulator (or muladd); (4) study FW's original MATLAB Padé impl (pre-GGT 2013) and port any per-step roundoff tricks. Probe: 10-step wedge walk at 22.5° amplifies O(eps) per-coefficient differences in h^k rescaling to 2.5e-10 in u — concentrated in the step crossing the z=1 lattice pole (~65× amplification alone). Order ≥ 30 saturates Float64 truncation; raising order does NOT close the gap. Suitable for a Phase-15 'accuracy hardening' subproject.
notes: Superseded by the new P1 bead for classical-Padé default in RobustPade.  The four attack vectors listed in this bead's description (Jacobi for F64, Kahan-summed Horner, compensated h^k, port FW's MATLAB Padé) all reduce to a single answer: FW used Toeplitz \ (linear solve, not SVD).  Worklog 020 documents the empirical probe (classical Padé closes the F64 gap to <1e-13 at z=30, <1e-10 at z=10⁴ — better than FW at z=10⁴).  Closing as superseded once the new bead is claimed.

title: Performance profiling pass
description: Full test suite wall ~2 min. Dominant tests: PN.2.2 BF-256 (~60s), Phase 9 tritronquée (~1s but 12 testsets), ext_arblib (~3s). No profiling done. Tasks: (a) BenchmarkTools.jl sweep across core operations (one Padé step, one Newton iteration, one path_network_solve segment); (b) identify hotspots; (c) optimise without breaking generic-T discipline; (d) re-run full suite to confirm wall-time reduction. Documentation: docs/perf-notes.md or worklog. Independent of correctness work; can run in parallel with other beads.

title: API review pass for consistency
description: Holistic review of public API across all 13 modules: (a) kwarg naming (h vs h_path vs h_max — currently inconsistent), (b) return-type consistency (Solution structs vs tuples vs raw arrays), (c) error message style (Suggestion line / Detail line / "got X" wording), (d) bang-suffix usage (! for mutating), (e) capitalization (snake_case throughout?). Produce a checklist of inconsistencies + a proposed pre-v1.0 normalisation. May produce breaking-change recommendations that are deferred to a v1.0 bead.

title: Add example scripts for non-PI modules
description: examples/ currently ships two PI tritronquée scripts (tritronquee_3d.jl, tritronquee_bvp_compose.jl). The other 12 architectural modules have no example scripts. Useful examples to add: (a) PIII pole field via CoordTransforms (FFW Fig 1-style); (b) PV tronquée via CoordTransforms (FFW Fig 4-style); (c) PVI multi-sheet via SheetTracker (FFW Fig 3-style); (d) FW Fig 4.1 BVP demo (visualisation of test/fw_fig_41_test.jl's BVP solution); (e) lattice_dispatch_solve on a simple cosh problem (validates the per-row BVP fill visually). Each example should be ~50-100 LOC, runnable via julia --project=examples examples/<name>.jl, PNG output to examples/ (gitignored).

title: Expand BigFloat-256 test coverage across modules
description: BF-256 paths are claimed generic (T <: AbstractFloat) but actual BF-256 tests are sparse. Worklog 006 lesson 7 noted BF-256 only buys precision past Float64's SVD-conditioning limit (order ≥ 40). Currently BF-256-tested: pathnetwork (PN.2.2 z=30), problems (test 6.1.6 BF-256 high-order), bvp (a few cases), ext_arblib. Untested at BF-256: CoordTransforms (Phase 13), SheetTracker (Phase 14), LatticeDispatcher, EdgeDetector, Dispatcher, much of PathNetwork beyond PN.2.2. Add representative BF-256 tests per module to validate the generic-T claim end-to-end. References: docs/worklog/006-phases-10-11-path-network-bvp.md lesson 7.

title: SheetTracker: η-plane PVI equation (FFW eq. 5)
description: Phase 14 follow-on (worklog 018 deferral). The second exponential ζ = e^η maps PVI's ζ=0 branch point out of the finite plane, leaving fixed singularities at η = log|2πk| + i·arg(2πi·k) for |k| ≥ 1. Useful for FFW Fig 2 column 1 only — the rest of the Tier-5 figures use the ζ-plane already shipped. Equation has nested e^(e^η) terms (FFW2017...md:154); ~40 LOC + 4 testsets. Hand-derive RHS carefully against md:154 verbatim. References: FFW2017...md:146-162 (the η-plane setup), docs/worklog/018-sheet-tracker-pVI.md §"What is NOT shipped".
